steps.title: Follow the causal chain
steps.content: The passage links three facts: Scottish English dialects emphasize the r sound, Upland South English dialects do the same, and the Upland South was largely colonized by people with Scottish ancestry. The conclusion should connect the shared feature to that settlement history.

steps.title: Avoid reversals and predictions
steps.content: Choice B predicts future spread, which the passage does not discuss. Choice C reverses the direction of influence. Choice D says Scottish settlers abandoned the feature, contradicting the similarity described. The logical conclusion is that the Upland South feature came from Scottish dialects. The answer is A.
